ogTitle: Olympic Dam vs Onkaparinga Hills: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Olympic Dam and Onkaparinga Hills.
heading: Olympic Dam vs Onkaparinga Hills — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Olympic Dam, South Australia and Onkaparinga Hills, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Onkaparinga Hills has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Olympic Dam (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Olympic Dam or Onkaparinga Hills safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Olympic Dam has a flood risk score of 46/100, while Onkaparinga Hills scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Olympic Dam scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. Onkaparinga Hills scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Olympic Dam or Onkaparinga Hills?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Onkaparinga Hills has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
